name: Physics.Body

aliases.name: Body
aliases.comment:
Represents a physical body containing
user defined data, like a WebGL mesh.

By default a body is positioned in the center
of the world and has zero mass. To change this,
use [setPosition](#setPosition) or [setMass](#setMass).
Bodies with zero mass don’t move!

The supported bodies are:

  - [box](#box),
  - [plane](#plane),
  - [sphere](#sphere).

For complex bodies check [compound](#compound).

values.name: box
values.comment:
A box is defined by its dimensions along the corresponding axes.
To create a 1x1 box, call this:

    box { x = 1, y = 1, z = 1 } data
values.type: { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } -> data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: compound
values.comment:
Make a compound body from a list of [shapes](Physics-Shape#Shape).

For example, the [sphere](#sphere) from above can be defined like this:

    sphere radius data =
        compound [ Shape.sphere radius ] data

We only support [rigid bodies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rigid_body).
values.type: List.List Physics.Shape.Shape -> data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: getData
values.comment: Get user-defined data.
values.type: Physics.Body.Body data -> data

values.name: getOrientation
values.comment: Get orientation as a unit quaternion.
values.type: Physics.Body.Body data -> { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float, w : Basics.Float }

values.name: getPosition
values.type: Physics.Body.Body data -> { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }

values.name: getTransformation
values.comment:
Get the position and the orientation of the body in a single matrix.

Elements are given by their row and column indices, starting at 1,
so `m23` means the element in the second row, third column.

To use this with WebGL, pass the result to [`Math.Matrix4.fromRecord`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm-explorations/linear-algebra/latest/Math-Matrix4#fromRecord).
values.type: Physics.Body.Body data -> { m11 : Basics.Float, m21 : Basics.Float, m31 : Basics.Float, m41 : Basics.Float, m12 : Basics.Float, m22 : Basics.Float, m32 : Basics.Float, m42 : Basics.Float, m13 : Basics.Float, m23 : Basics.Float, m33 : Basics.Float, m43 : Basics.Float, m14 : Basics.Float, m24 : Basics.Float, m34 : Basics.Float, m44 : Basics.Float }

values.name: moveBy
values.comment: Move the body by a vector offset from the current position.
values.type: { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: plane
values.comment:
A plane with the normal that points
in the direction of the z axis.

A plane is collidable in the direction of the normal.
values.type: data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: rotateBy
values.comment:
Rotate the body by a specific angle around the axis
from the current orientation. Angle must be specified in radians.
values.type: Basics.Float -> { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: setData
values.comment: Set user-defined data.
values.type: data -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: setMass
values.comment: Set the body mass. Bodies with zero mass don’t move!
values.type: Basics.Float -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: setMaterial
values.comment: Set the [material](Physics-Material) to controll friction and bounciness.
values.type: Physics.Material.Material -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: setOrientation
values.comment: Set the body orientation to a [unit quaternion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quaternions_and_spatial_rotation).
values.type: { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float, w : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: setPosition
values.comment: Set the absolute position of the body in the world.
values.type: { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data

values.name: sphere
values.comment: A sphere is defined by its radius.
values.type: Basics.Float -> data -> Physics.Body.Body data

aliases.name: Constraint
aliases.comment:
Constraint allows to limit the freedom of movement
of two bodies with relation to each other.

values.name: hinge
values.comment:
Keep two bodies connected with each other like pointToPoint,
but also limit the freedom of rotation. Useful for e.g. connecting
a window to a window frame.
values.type: { pivot1 : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }, axis1 : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }, pivot2 : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }, axis2 : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } } -> Physics.Constraint.Constraint

values.name: pointToPoint
values.comment:
Connect a point on the first body with a point on the second body.
Points are defined within the bodies’ local coordinate systems.
values.type: { pivot1 : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }, pivot2 : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } } -> Physics.Constraint.Constraint

name: Physics.Debug

aliases.name: FaceNormal
aliases.comment:
A face normal consists of a normal vector for a face
and a reference point on the face.

These are both expressed within the local body coordinate system.
aliases.type: { normal : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }, point : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } }

aliases.name: UniqueEdge
aliases.comment:
A unique edge consists of a unit direction vector
that runs parallel to an edge of a face.

A vertex point of the body is also provided for context.

These are both expressed within the local body coordinate system.
aliases.type: { direction : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }, point : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } }

values.name: getContacts
values.comment: Get the contact points in the world.
values.type: Physics.World.World data -> List.List { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }

values.name: getFaceNormals
values.comment: Get the face normals of the body.
values.type: Physics.Body.Body data -> List.List Physics.Debug.FaceNormal

values.name: getUniqueEdges
values.comment: Get the unique edges of the body.
values.type: Physics.Body.Body data -> List.List Physics.Debug.UniqueEdge

aliases.name: Material
aliases.comment:
Materials allow to control friction and bounciness.
You can change materials using [Body.setMaterial](Physics-Body#setMaterial).

values.name: custom
values.comment:
Creates a custom material, e.g.

    slippery =
        custom { friction = 0, bounciness = 0 }

    bouncy =
        custom { friction = 0.3, bounciness = 0.9 }

When two materials collide, their properties are averaged
and clamped between 0 and 1.
values.type: { friction : Basics.Float, bounciness : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Material.Material

values.name: default
values.type: Physics.Material.Material

name: Physics.Shape

aliases.name: Shape
aliases.comment:
Shapes are only needed for creating [compound](Physics-Body#compound) bodies.

If you need a body with a single shape, use the corresponding functions
from the [Physics.Body](Physics-Body) module.

The supported shapes are:

  - [box](#box),
  - [plane](#plane),
  - [sphere](#sphere).

values.name: box
values.comment: A box is defined by dimensions along the corresponding axes.
values.type: { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Shape.Shape

values.name: moveBy
values.comment:
Move the shape in a body by a vector offset from
the current local position.
values.type: { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Shape.Shape -> Physics.Shape.Shape

values.name: plane
values.comment:
A plane with the normal that points in the
direction of the z axis.
values.type: Physics.Shape.Shape

values.name: rotateBy
values.comment:
Rotate the shape in a body by a specific angle
around the axis from the current local orientation.
values.type: Basics.Float -> { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Shape.Shape -> Physics.Shape.Shape

values.name: setOrientation
values.comment: Set the local shape orientation to a [unit quaternion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quaternions_and_spatial_rotation).
values.type: { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float, w : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Shape.Shape -> Physics.Shape.Shape

values.name: setPosition
values.comment: Set the local position of the shape in a body.
values.type: { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } -> Physics.Shape.Shape -> Physics.Shape.Shape

values.name: sphere
values.comment: A sphere is defined by a radius and a position.
values.type: Basics.Float -> Physics.Shape.Shape

name: Physics.World

aliases.name: RaycastResult
aliases.comment:
The Raycast result includes the intersected body,
intersection point and normal vector on the face,
expressed within the local body coordinate system.
aliases.type: { body : Physics.Body.Body data, point : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }, normal : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } }

aliases.name: World
aliases.comment: Physical world is our abstract playground for physical simulations.

values.name: add
values.comment:
Adds a body to the world.

    worldWithFloor =
        add planeBody world

Check the [Physics.Body](Physics-Body) module for possible bodies.
values.type: Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: constrain
values.comment:
Configure constraints between pairs of bodies. Constraints allow to limit the
freedom of movement of two bodies with relation to each other.

Check the [Physics.Constraint](Physics-Constraint) module for possible constraints.

    worldWithACar : World { part : String }
    worldWithACar =
        constrain
            (\b1 b2 ->
                case ( (Body.getData b1).part, (Body.getData b2).part ) of
                    ( "wheel1", "base" ) ->
                        [ hingeConstraint1 ]

                    ( "wheel2", "base" ) ->
                        [ hingeConstraint2 ]

                    ( "wheel3", "base" ) ->
                        [ hingeConstraint3 ]

                    ( "wheel4", "base" ) ->
                        [ hingeConstraint4 ]

                    _ ->
                        []
            )
            worldWithCarParts

Note that this example only works for a single car, otherwise it would
connect wheels of one car with the base of another another. You might want
to use `constrainIf` to apply constraints on a subset of bodies.

    constrain =
        constrainIf (always True)
values.type: (Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data -> List.List Physics.Constraint.Constraint) -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: constrainIf
values.comment:
Configure constraints for a subset of bodies that satisfy the test.

For the above example we can tag each part of a car with the `carId`,
and preselect parts of a single car with:

    constrainCar carId =
        constrainIf (\body -> (Body.getData body).carId == carId)
values.type: (Physics.Body.Body data -> Basics.Bool) -> (Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data -> List.List Physics.Constraint.Constraint) -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: empty
values.comment: Let be the new world!
values.type: Physics.World.World data

values.name: getBodies
values.comment:
Get all bodies from the world.

Use this to convert bodies into visual representation,
e.g. WebGL entities.
values.type: Physics.World.World data -> List.List (Physics.Body.Body data)

values.name: keepIf
values.comment: Keep bodies that satisfy the test.
values.type: (Physics.Body.Body data -> Basics.Bool) -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: raycast
values.comment:
Find the closest intersection of a ray against
all the bodies in the world.
values.type: { from : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float }, direction : { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } } -> Physics.World.World data -> Maybe.Maybe (Physics.World.RaycastResult data)

values.name: setGravity
values.comment:
Set the [standard gravity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_gravity), e.g.:

    planetEarth =
        setGravity { x = 0, y = 0, z = -9.80665 } world
values.type: { x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, z : Basics.Float } -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: simulate
values.comment:
Simulate the world, given the number of milliseconds since the last frame.

Call this function on a message from the [onAnimationFrame](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/elm/browser/latest/Browser-Events#onAnimationFrame) subscription.
values.type: Basics.Float -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data

values.name: update
values.comment: Apply a function to every body in the world.
values.type: (Physics.Body.Body data -> Physics.Body.Body data) -> Physics.World.World data -> Physics.World.World data
